compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Reproduction Steps
1. Mount the tickets store and run `rebar verify-identity` against the real store (~3504 events, ~29,575 tracker commits).
2. Observe the command does not complete within 280s (locally exit 124 / timeout).

**Expected:** the merge-gate completes in seconds.

**Actual:** the report loop calls `rebar.attest.authorship.resolve_event_commit` once per non-verified event, and that function runs `git log --diff-filter=A --format=%H -- <pathspec>` — a full-history traversal — per event. With N events and H commits it is O(N x H): 3504 x 29575 here. A bounded `--base HEAD~200` scan also exceeded 180s.

## Root Cause
`src/rebar/_commands/verify_authorship.py` cli() loop: for every non-`verified` event it calls `_resolve_commit` -> `authorship.resolve_event_commit` (`src/rebar/attest/authorship.py`), one `git log` subprocess over the whole tracker history each. Enforced-vs-grandfathered adds a `git merge-base --is-ancestor` subprocess per event on top.

## Fix Sketch
Resolve introducing commits in a SINGLE pass: one `git log --diff-filter=A --name-only --format=%H` over the tracker, build a {event-file-path -> commit} map, and look up per event (O(N + H)). Keep the per-event path as fail-closed fallback. Add a perf regression test at representative scale.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Introducing-commit resolution for a full-store scan is single-pass (not one git-log per event).
- [ ] `rebar verify-identity` over a store of ~3.5k events / ~30k commits completes in well under a minute.
- [ ] A test asserts the batched resolver returns the same commit SHAs as the per-event resolver on a seeded store.
- [ ] Fail-closed behaviour (unresolvable commit -> enforced) is preserved.
compiled_state.title: verify-identity gate is O(events x history): one git-log per event makes CI multi-minute
